text.title: Stakeholder Management: Register, Communication Plan, and Examples
text.summary: Stakeholder register column schema, communication plan matrix, meeting template structure, and worked examples for both SaaS feature launch and solo product contexts.

rule.statement: The register must include these columns: name, role, interest (H/M/L), power (H/M/L), attitude (Supporter/Neutral/Resistor/Unknown), engagement level (Partner/Involved/Informed/Monitor), owner. The owner column is non-optional — "whole team manages this exec" means nobody does.
rule.rationale: Without a named owner per stakeholder relationship, escalations have no clear path and engagement drifts to whoever is most available rather than most informed.

rule.statement: Cite the source for every Power and Attitude classification (meeting notes, org chart, repo CODEOWNERS). Mark Unknown if no source exists, not a guess.
rule.rationale: LLM-generated stakeholder analyses hallucinate stakeholders from project-type templates. Requiring a source for each classification catches fabricated rows before they drive engagement decisions.

list.item: Executive sponsor: status + decisions, weekly, 1:1 meeting, PM owns
Dev team: requirements + changes, daily, stand-up, PM owns
Sales team: features + timeline, bi-weekly, email update, PM owns
Customers: releases + feedback, monthly, newsletter, Marketing owns

rule.statement: Define the escalation path with real names and backups: Issue → PM → (24h unresolved) → Director → (48h unresolved) → Executive sponsor. "Escalate to Director" with no name is unactionable at 11pm.
rule.rationale: Escalation paths with role names instead of person names fail when roles turn over or when multiple people hold the role. Named individuals with backup contacts are actionable; role labels are not.

example.note: SaaS feature launch (5+ stakeholders): CEO (High/Medium → Keep Satisfied, quarterly), Head of Sales (High/High → Partner, weekly sync), Engineering Lead (Medium/High → Involved, daily standups), Key Customer (Low/High → Informed, beta access), Support Team (Low/High → Involved, training sessions). Explicit owner assigned to each row.

Solo product stakeholder register: Early customers (need working product + support → Discord and email), Payment processor (need compliance → documentation), Hosting provider (need technical requirements → support tickets), Advisor/mentor (need progress → monthly call), Family (need time boundaries → clear schedule). Even solopreneurs have stakeholders requiring explicit engagement plans.

Register with attitude column left blank for all rows, owner column filled with "team", and cadence listed as "ad-hoc". This register produces no value — attitude Unknown rows never get escalated, no one owns relationships, and "ad-hoc" means nothing happens.

text.reference: Register with all required columns and detailed profile section
Communication matrix, key messages by audience, escalation path
Stakeholder update meeting agenda: progress, decisions needed, risks, action items
